sources.nws: https://api.weather.gov (gridpoint hourly forecast, queried for the game-time hour at each park's coordinates)

notes: NWS wind direction is 'wind FROM' compass direction (meteorological convention); RotoWire's 'In'/'Out' is relative to the park's orientation (wind blowing toward/away from the field). These aren't directly comparable without a per-park orientation mapping -- deferred until it's clear this data is worth deploying.

Toronto (Rogers Centre) is a dome AND outside NWS's coverage area (US-only) -- any future Blue Jays home game would need a different source regardless of dome status. Real, permanent limitation of the NWS approach.
KC@DET shows a real 5F gap between RotoWire (73) and NWS (78) captured at the same hour -- worth explaining once MLB's actual reading is available tonight.
